# Scenario: MCP (Model Context Protocol) generic tool renderer
#
# Why this matters:
#   MCP tools are named `<server>_<tool>' or `<server>-<tool>'
#   (e.g. `tiksearch_mcp__tiksearch', `lark-cli_send-message').
#   The chat buffer has no tool-specific renderer for them — instead
#   it falls through to a generic renderer that prints each INPUT
#   key as a `  key: value' line and then the tool output.
#
#   Two invariants this scenario guards:
#
#   1. MCP tools are collapsed-by-default (like Bash/Read) so the diff
#      or huge JSON output doesn't dominate the chat.
#   2. The generic input renderer must handle every JSON type the
#      server sends: string, number, boolean, null, nested object,
#      array (homogeneous and mixed).  Without this fallback, MCP tool
#      calls would show the cryptic `▸ tool-name' header with nothing
#      else.

:session ses_mcp_render
:directory /tmp/mcp-render-test

# --- User message ---
:sse {"directory":"/tmp/mcp-render-test","payload":{"type":"message.updated","properties":{"info":{"id":"msg_u1","sessionID":"ses_mcp_render","role":"user","time":{"created":1770000000000}}}}}

# --- Session busy ---
:sse {"directory":"/tmp/mcp-render-test","payload":{"type":"session.status","properties":{"sessionID":"ses_mcp_render","status":{"type":"busy"}}}}
:assert-busy

# --- Assistant message ---
:sse {"directory":"/tmp/mcp-render-test","payload":{"type":"message.updated","properties":{"info":{"id":"msg_a1","sessionID":"ses_mcp_render","role":"assistant","time":{"created":1770000000100},"parentID":"msg_u1","modelID":"claude-opus-4-6","providerID":"anthropic","mode":"build","tokens":{"input":0,"output":0,"reasoning":0,"cache":{"read":0,"write":0}}}}}}

# --- Step start ---
:sse {"directory":"/tmp/mcp-render-test","payload":{"type":"message.part.updated","properties":{"part":{"id":"prt_step1","sessionID":"ses_mcp_render","messageID":"msg_a1","type":"step-start"}}}}

# --- MCP tool call: tiksearch_mcp__tiksearch with a rich input plist ---
:sse {"directory":"/tmp/mcp-render-test","payload":{"type":"message.part.updated","properties":{"part":{"id":"prt_mcp1","sessionID":"ses_mcp_render","messageID":"msg_a1","type":"tool","callID":"call_mcp_001","tool":"tiksearch_mcp__tiksearch","state":{"status":"completed","input":{"userQuery":"how does feature X work","limit":5,"recent":true,"cursor":null,"nested":{"key":"value","count":3},"tags":["fast","cheap","long"]},"output":"[{\"title\":\"doc1\"},{\"title\":\"doc2\"}]","metadata":{},"time":{"start":1770000000200,"end":1770000000500}}}}}}

# --- Session idle ---
:sse {"directory":"/tmp/mcp-render-test","payload":{"type":"session.status","properties":{"sessionID":"ses_mcp_render","status":{"type":"idle"}}}}
:sse {"directory":"/tmp/mcp-render-test","payload":{"type":"session.idle","properties":{"sessionID":"ses_mcp_render"}}}
:assert-idle

# --- Assertions ---
# Tool section header is present.
:assert-contains tiksearch_mcp__tiksearch

# MCP tools are EXPANDED by default (unlike bash/read which collapse)
# so the user can see the arguments without opening the section.
# The `[collapsed]' indicator must NOT be on the MCP tool's header.
# The simplest check: the body key `userQuery:' must be visible without
# toggling the section.
:assert-not-contains [collapsed]

# String value rendered.
:assert-contains userQuery: how does feature X work

# Numeric value.
:assert-contains limit: 5

# Boolean true.
:assert-contains recent: true

# Null value.
:assert-contains cursor: null

# Nested object rendered compactly.
:assert-contains nested: {key: value, count: 3}

# Array rendered as [a, b, c].
:assert-contains tags: [fast, cheap, long]
